fields.summary: Represents a four-byte tag used to identify font tables and other Skia resources.
fields.remarks:
<format type="text/markdown"><![CDATA[
## Remarks

`SKFourByteTag` is a compact identifier made of four ASCII characters packed into a
`uint`. It is used throughout Skia to identify font tables (such as `'wght'`, `'wdth'`),
variation axes, and other resources.

You can create an `SKFourByteTag` from a `uint`, from four individual characters, or
by parsing a string with <xref:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag.Parse(System.String)>. When
parsing, strings shorter than four characters are padded with spaces; strings longer
than four characters are truncated to four.

This is a value type and does not require disposal.

## Examples

```csharp
var weightTag = SKFourByteTag.Parse("wght");
var widthTag = new SKFourByteTag('w', 'd', 't', 'h');
Console.WriteLine(weightTag.ToString()); // "wght"
```
]]></format>

Parses a string into an <see cref="T:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag" />.

params.tag: A string of characters. Strings shorter than four characters are padded with spaces; strings longer than four are truncated to four. A <see langword="null" /> or empty string returns a tag with value 0.

fields.returns: A four-character string decoded from the packed tag value.
fields.summary: Returns the four-character string representation of this tag.
